warnings: Dropped 2 non-scored row(s) worth 0 points (Penalty Points (5 points for dress code penalty), Penalty Points (5 points for late arrival penalty)). Check they really aren't scored.
_review.note: Machine-parsed from the official rating sheet. plan.md F3: nobody may be graded against this until a human confirms it.
_review.paraphrase_note: Descriptions and level descriptors are restated in our own words (plan.md §20). Criterion names and every point value are exactly as published on the official rating sheet.

criteria.name: Main Motion
criteria.description: The accurate classification of main motions as demonstrated during the role-play scenario.

levels.label: Not Completed
levels.descriptor: The main motion was not attempted or was entirely incorrect.

levels.label: Completed
levels.descriptor: The main motion was attempted, but its application or classification had significant errors, earning between 1 and 6 points.

levels.label: Completed & Correct
levels.descriptor: The main motion was correctly identified and applied, earning between 7 and 10 points.

criteria.name: Subsidiary Motion
criteria.description: The accurate identification and use of subsidiary motions as presented in the role-play.

levels.label: Not Completed
levels.descriptor: This motion type was not used or was applied incorrectly.

levels.label: Completed
levels.descriptor: The motion was introduced, but its execution or classification contained notable inaccuracies, resulting in 1 to 6 points.

levels.label: Completed & Correct
levels.descriptor: The motion was correctly classified and utilized, achieving 7 to 10 points.

criteria.name: Privileged Motion
criteria.description: The correct classification of privileged motions as demonstrated within the role-play.

levels.label: Not Completed
levels.descriptor: No privileged motion was presented, or it was completely misapplied.

levels.label: Completed
levels.descriptor: A privileged motion was attempted, but its classification or use showed considerable flaws, scoring 1 to 6 points.

levels.label: Completed & Correct
levels.descriptor: The privileged motion was accurately classified and implemented, earning 7 to 10 points.

criteria.name: Incidental Motion
criteria.description: The accurate categorization of incidental motions as depicted in the role-play.

levels.label: Not Completed
levels.descriptor: The incidental motion was absent or incorrectly handled.

levels.label: Completed
levels.descriptor: An incidental motion was introduced, but its classification or execution contained significant errors, receiving 1 to 6 points.

levels.label: Completed & Correct
levels.descriptor: The incidental motion was correctly classified and applied, meriting 7 to 10 points.

criteria.name: Bring Again Motion
criteria.description: The appropriate classification and handling of motions that bring a question before the assembly again, as presented in the role-play.

levels.label: O points
levels.descriptor: This type of motion was not utilized or was used entirely incorrectly.

levels.label: 1-6 points
levels.descriptor: The motion was attempted, but its application or classification contained errors, resulting in 1 to 6 points.

levels.label: 7-10 points
levels.descriptor: The motion was correctly identified and applied, earning 7 to 10 points.

criteria.name: Call to Order
criteria.description: The proper initiation of the meeting's proceedings.

levels.label: O points
levels.descriptor: The meeting was not called to order or was done so incorrectly.

levels.label: 1-3 points
levels.descriptor: The call to order was attempted but contained noticeable flaws, earning 1 to 3 points.

levels.label: 4-5 points
levels.descriptor: The meeting was properly called to order, meriting 4 to 5 points.

criteria.name: Problem Addressed
criteria.description: The extent to which the core issue of the meeting was identified and discussed.

levels.label: 0 points
levels.descriptor: The central problem was not identified or was completely misunderstood.

levels.label: 1-16 points
levels.descriptor: The problem was partially addressed, but with significant gaps or inaccuracies, scoring 1 to 16 points.

levels.label: 17-25 points
levels.descriptor: The problem was thoroughly and accurately addressed, earning 17 to 25 points.

criteria.name: Proper Use of Parliamentary Terms
criteria.description: The correct application of parliamentary language and procedures during the meeting.

levels.label: 0 points
levels.descriptor: Parliamentary terms were not used or were used entirely incorrectly.

levels.label: 1-6 points
levels.descriptor: Some parliamentary terms were used, but with frequent errors or inappropriate contexts, earning 1 to 6 points.

levels.label: 7-10 points
levels.descriptor: Parliamentary terms were consistently and correctly applied throughout the meeting, meriting 7 to 10 points.

criteria.name: Voices Projected and Expressions Clear
criteria.description: The audibility of participants' voices and the clarity of their verbal expressions.

levels.label: O points
levels.descriptor: Voices were not projected, and expressions were unclear, making communication difficult.

levels.label: 1-3 points
levels.descriptor: Voices were somewhat projected, and expressions were mostly clear, but with occasional lapses, earning 1 to 3 points.

levels.label: 4-5 points
levels.descriptor: Voices were consistently projected, and expressions were clear and understandable, meriting 4 to 5 points.

criteria.name: Presiding Official Remained Impartial
criteria.description: The presiding officer's ability to maintain neutrality and avoid bias throughout the meeting.

levels.label: 0 points
levels.descriptor: The presiding official showed clear partiality or interfered inappropriately.

levels.label: 1-3 points
levels.descriptor: The presiding official generally remained impartial but showed minor instances of bias or intervention, earning 1 to 3 points.

levels.label: 4-5 points
levels.descriptor: The presiding official maintained strict impartiality and facilitated the meeting fairly, meriting 4 to 5 points.

criteria.name: Members took initiative in meeting
criteria.description: The active engagement and self-starting actions of the meeting participants.

levels.label: 0 points
levels.descriptor: Members showed no initiative or participation in the meeting.

levels.label: 1-3 points
levels.descriptor: Members displayed some initiative, but their contributions were limited or hesitant, earning 1 to 3 points.

levels.label: 4-5 points
levels.descriptor: Members actively took initiative, contributing meaningfully and driving the meeting forward, meriting 4 to 5 points.

criteria.name: Meeting Adjournment
criteria.description: The correct and formal conclusion of the meeting.

levels.label: 0 points
levels.descriptor: The meeting was not adjourned or was done so incorrectly.

levels.label: 1-3 points
levels.descriptor: The adjournment was attempted but contained noticeable flaws, earning 1 to 3 points.

levels.label: 4-5 points
levels.descriptor: The meeting was properly adjourned, meriting 4 to 5 points.

criteria.name: Adherence to Competitive Events Guidelines
criteria.description: Compliance with all established rules and instructions for the competitive event.

levels.label: Competitor(s) Did Not Follow Guidelines
levels.descriptor: The competitor(s) failed to adhere to the specified guidelines for the event.

levels.label: Execution Aligned with Guidelines
levels.descriptor: The presentation was consistent with the assigned topic. No materials were left behind after the presentation. Only items explicitly permitted by the guidelines were brought into the preparation and presentation rooms.
